rulings.Case Title: ZOILA ACOSTA MURCIA ET AL VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 6, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. and RECONTRUST COMPANY N.A.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues continued to June 10, 2016 per June 2, 2016 ex-parte order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIANA TRUST, A DIVISION OF WILMINGTON VS. TASNEE BUNCHIEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 6, 2016, line 2. DEFENDANT ALICIA SHERIDAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT overruled, complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN C. HUNT ET AL VS. SAMANTHA MARIKO MIRANDA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 6, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT SAMANTHA MIRANDA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, Notice is sufficient re: SFRO 37.9(a)(13); 37.9A(f)(5)(e) and Govt. Code 7060(b)(1)(B). Moving party raises issues of fact not appropriate for demurrer. There is no indication in the complaint that Storage Rooms occupied by Stephen Glenn are "housing services, privileges, furnishings and facilities supplied in connection with the use or occupancy" of unit 1964 Larkin Street occupied by Robert Dodd; or that the Storage Rooms are accommodations within the meaning of Ellis Act or rental unit within the meaning of SFRO. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN C. HUNT ET AL VS. SAMANTHA MARIKO MIRANDA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 6, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT SAMANTHA MIRANDA Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Complaint DENIED. Inclusion of Election of Forfeiture in the notice is not necessary in the unlawful detainers not based on non-payment of rent in order to pray for forfeiture in the complaint. See Rutter Guide, Landlord-Tenant, 7:147 and 8:77. Additionally, the complaint properly alleges a value of the use of the property that defendant is allegedly unlawfully detaining and the prayer for relief seeks a proper form of damages in and unlawful detainer action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AUGUSTO CANO VS. ALEJANDRO CLEMENTE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 6, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANTS ALEJANDRO CLEMENTE and CARMEN DELGADO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint and Notice are sufficient to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer. = (501/REQ)
